basis: CIF NWE / Dated Brent physical proxy
note: Brent has pushed toward $99 (about $98.75), up more than 30% this month, as the war premium builds — earlier-week ceasefire hopes have fully reversed. U.S. strikes have run a twelfth consecutive night, and the Red Sea threat is no longer hypothetical but biting: the Houthi embargo on ships calling at Saudi ports has forced Saudi-crude cargoes to divert toward Suez and left several Yanbu-bound tankers paused in the Arabian Sea, the EU's ASPIDES mission has raised the North Red Sea to a Medium threat, and Yanbu was down to two of seven export berths — the “two-chokepoint problem” (Standard Chartered), with Hormuz still at a near-halt. Physical NWE differentials remain firm with the benchmark, and refined-product tightness stays acute — European low-sulphur gasoil near a record ~$60/bbl premium over Brent after Russia's export ban. Editorial proxy, not a traded quote — indicative, and watch tanker-transit data over the rhetoric.
